recalls.Description:
IBC Technologies has issued a recall for specific models of its CX Series Combi Boilers, which are designed for residential use to provide both central heating and hot water. These boilers, identifiable by their rectangular metal design and a control panel on the front, are at risk of overheating water beyond the set temperature, creating a burn hazard for users. The recall affects models CX-199 and CX-150 with specific serial numbers manufactured between April 2023 and April 2025.

Consumers should check the model and serial numbers located on the rating plate inside the unit to determine if their boiler is included in the recall. IBC Technologies is committed to ensuring safety and will provide necessary repairs to affected units.
recalls.Title: IBC Technologies Recalls CX Series Combi Boilers Due to Potential Burn Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact IBC at 844-432-8422 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email sales@ibcboiler.com, or visit www.ibcboiler.com/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: CX Series Combi Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,100 (In addition, about 880 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Location of Model Number and Serial Number on the Rating Plate Inside the Recalled CX Combi Boiler

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the hot water temperature fluctuating while in use. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through home improvement distributors nationwide from May 2023 to June 2025, priced between $4,700 and $5,100

Importers.Name: IBC Technologies USA Inc., of Lawnside, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to overheating water

Remedies.Name: Free in-home repair by an authorized technician, including installation of a mixing valve if necessary

recalls.metaDescription: IBC Technologies recalls CX Series Combi Boilers due to burn hazards. Check if your model is affected and learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: NTI is recalling its TRX Series II residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically the models TRX150, TRX199, and TRX199C. These boilers are gray, measuring approximately 20 inches in width and 34 inches in height. The relevant model and serial numbers can be found on the rating plate located on the left side of the boiler. The affected serial number ranges are as follows: TRX150 (3314306C8: 242830000177-250130000073), TRX199 (3314307C8: 242740000006-250220000014), and TRX199C (3314308C8: 242750000127-250410000051).
recalls.Title: Recall of NTI TRX Series II Boilers Due to Fire and Carbon Monoxide Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach NTI Boilers at 800-688-2575 from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@ntiboilers.com, or visit http://www.ntiboilers.com/trx-safety-recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.ntiboilers.com and click on 'Urgent Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: NTI TRX150 Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 323 (In addition, 459 were sold in Canada)

Retailers.Name: Sold through independent heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors nationwide from September 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,400 and $4,100.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning due to improper sealing of the boiler door.

Remedies.Name: Corrective measures to ensure proper sealing of the boiler door.

RemedyOptions.Option: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled boilers and contact NTI Boilers to arrange to have a certified technician install the repair at no cost to the consumer. Consumers who must continue using the boiler while awaiting repair should have working smoke and carbon monoxide alarms in their homes.

recalls.metaDescription: NTI recalls TRX Series II gas boilers due to fire and CO poisoning hazards. Learn more about the recall and safety measures.

recalls.Description: HTP has issued a recall for its ELU Elite Ultra residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically models ELU-150WBN and ELU-199WBN. These boilers, which are black and measure approximately 20 inches wide by 34 inches high, may have a faulty door seal due to incorrect burner door studs. This defect can allow flames and harmful gases to escape, posing significant fire and carbon monoxide poisoning hazards that could lead to serious injury or death. The model and serial numbers can be found on the rating plate located on the left side of the unit.
recalls.Title: HTP ELU Elite Ultra Gas Boilers Recalled for Fire and Carbon Monoxide Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled boilers immediately and contact HTP at 800-323-9651, available from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday. Additional information can be obtained via email at recall@htproducts.com or online at https://htproducts.com/safety-notice.html. Visit www.htproducts.com and click on 'Urgent Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: HTP ELU Elite Ultra 150WBN Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 96 (In addition, 9 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled HTP-brand ELU Elite Ultra boiler

Retailers.Name: Sold through independent heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors across the U.S. from October 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,400 and $4,100.

Importers.Name: Ariston USA LLC, of Providence, Rhode Island

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning due to improper sealing of the boiler door.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the boilers and contact HTP for a free repair installation by a certified technician. If continued use is necessary before repair, ensure working smoke and carbon monoxide alarms are present.

recalls.metaDescription: HTP recalls ELU Elite Ultra gas boilers due to fire and CO risks. Contact HTP for free repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: Rinnai America is recalling its i-Series Plus residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically models IP175S, IP199S, IP175199C, and IP199199C. These boilers feature a white label indicating they are MADE IN JAPAN, along with model numbers, a reference code, and serial numbers. The rectangular metal units display the Rinnai name prominently and include a temperature display and keypad on the front.
recalls.Title: Rinnai America Issues Recall for i-Series Plus Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Rinnai America Corporation at 800-621-9419 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, email recall@rinnai.us, or visit https://www.rinnai.us/support/recalls. Additional information is available on their website under the "Recall" section.

Products.Name: Rinnai i-Series Plus Gas Boilers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,030 (In addition, 249 were sold in Canada)

Retailers.Name: Available through independent heating and plumbing contractors, wholesale distributors nationwide, and online retailers including Amazon.com, Homedepot.com, and Lowes.com from September 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,900 and $5,000.

Importers.Name: Rinnai America Corporation, of Peachtree City, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to potential failure of the boiler door seal.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled boilers and contact Rinnai for a free repair by a certified technician. If continued use is necessary, ensure carbon monoxide alarms are installed on every level of the home and near sleeping areas.

recalls.metaDescription: Rinnai recalls i-Series Plus boilers due to carbon monoxide risks. Stop using immediately and contact Rinnai for free repairs.

recalls.Description:
Triangle Tube is recalling its Prestige Solo, Prestige Excellence, and Aerco Esteem gas boilers due to a serious carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning hazard. These wall-hung condensing boilers are housed in a white metal casing, with 'Prestige' or 'Aerco' printed on the front panel. Consumers are urged to check their boiler's serial number against the recall list to determine if their unit is affected. The serial number can be found on the Boiler Rating Label on the front panel or on the right side of the unit.

The recall includes multiple models manufactured between December 2011 and April 2019. Consumers should take immediate action to ensure their safety, as the boilers may produce CO due to delayed ignitions or damaged components, which can lead to severe health risks or fatalities.
recalls.Title: Triangle Tube Recalls Prestige and Aerco Esteem Gas Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Triangle Tube at 877-574-5036 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at productrecall@triangletube.com. More information is available at https://triangletube.com/products/product-recall/ or by visiting www.triangletube.com and selecting 'PRODUCT RECALL.'

Products.Name: Prestige Solo Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: Approximately 60,000 (In addition, about 7,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Aerco Esteem Watts Brand Boiler

Injuries.Name: Triangle Tube has received two reports of death due to CO poisoning associated with the recalled boilers. In 2015, a 47-year-old woman died from CO poisoning associated with a Prestige Solo 175 boiler, and in 2016, an 85-year-old man died from CO poisoning associated with a Prestige SOLO TriMax 175 boiler, following a repair. Triangle Tube has also received 25 reports of CO leaks from the recalled boilers.

Retailers.Name: These boilers were produced by Burnsen SA in Belgium and sold through wholesale distributors and independent contractors across the country, with prices ranging from $3,400 to $9,700.

Importers.Name: Triangle Tube/Phase III Co. Inc., of West Deptford, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to potential delayed ignition or component failure.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Triangle Tube for a free inspection and, if needed, free installation of a new ignition system. Those using the recalled boilers should have functioning carbon monoxide alarms in their homes and can request a free alarm from Triangle Tube.

recalls.metaDescription: Triangle Tube recalls Prestige and Aerco Esteem gas boilers due to carbon monoxide risks. Check your unit and contact for a free inspection.

recalls.Description: Crown Boiler Company is recalling its Aruba IV (AWR Series) gas-fired hot water boilers, specifically those intended for use at altitudes exceeding 2,000 feet. These boilers, which are red and gray and measure 39.5 inches in height, do not display the model name on their labels. The model number can be found on the rating plate located on the top right side of the unit. Affected model numbers include a range from AWR070BLST3P8U to AWR280ENST2P8, regardless of their manufacturing date. Consumers should be aware that these boilers may pose a serious carbon monoxide poisoning hazard if the vent system becomes blocked.
recalls.Title: Crown Boiler Recalls Aruba IV Gas-Fired Hot Water Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Crown Boiler Company at 888-583-5624 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email AKP@velocityboilerworks.com or visit www.velocityboilerworks.com/news/2024/01/product-modification-required-crown-aruba-iv-models-above-2000-ft-recall/ for further details. Additionally, information is available at www.velocityboilerworks.com under "Product Recall Alerts Important Information" on the homepage.

Products.Name: Crown Aruba IV (AWR Series) Gas-Fired Hot Water Boilers

Images.Caption: Recalled Crown Boiler gas-fired AWR Series
Label location on recalled Crown Boiler gas-fired AWR Series
Model number location on rating plate of recalled Crown Boiler

Manufacturers.Name: Crown Boiler Company LLC, of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Available through heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors nationwide from July 2012 to December 2023, priced between $2,000 and $4,000

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to potential failure of the blocked vent switch

Remedies.Name: Free repair service including replacement of the main burner gas orifice and blocked vent switch if necessary

recalls.metaDescription: Crown Boiler recalls Aruba IV gas-fired hot water boilers due to carbon monoxide risks. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: U.S. Boiler Company is recalling several models of gas-fired residential boilers due to a potential carbon monoxide poisoning hazard. This recall affects boilers installed at elevations exceeding 5,400 feet and includes models from U.S. Boiler, New Yorker Boiler, Advantage, FORCE, and Archer. Specific models include the U.S. Boiler ES2 and Series 3, which feature a blue body with a black top and vents, as well as the X-2 model in solid teal, the gray Lancer model, and various models from New Yorker and Advantage in different colors. The model and serial numbers can be found on the rating plate located on the boiler's top panel or inside the vestibule behind a removable door.
recalls.Title: U.S. Boiler Company Recalls Gas-Fired Residential Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach U.S. Boiler at 888-432-8887 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@usboiler.net. More information is available online at https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catsy.782/high+altitude+homeowner+recall+letter3.pdf, usboiler.net, archerboiler.com, or newyorkerboiler.com by selecting "Safety Recall Information: High Altitude Gas Boilers" on the homepage.

Images.Caption: Rating Plate with arrow to serial number and model information

Manufacturers.Name: U.S. Boiler Company LLC, of Lancaster, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Sold through independent heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors across the U.S. from February 2009 to November 2023, priced between $2,000 and $4,000.

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to potential failure of the blocked vent switch at high altitudes

Remedies.Name: Free repair including replacement of the main burner gas orifice and blocked vent switch if necessary

recalls.metaDescription: U.S. Boiler recalls gas-fired residential boilers due to carbon monoxide risks. Contact for free repairs and safety information.
